articles.title: Prove 'poaching' charge or face ₹100 crore defamation suit: BJP to Omar Abdullah
articles.sum: "The BJP Jammu and Kashmir has sent a legal notice to Chief Minister Omar Abdullah through post/mail, demanding that he either provide concrete evidence to substantiate his allegations of 'poaching' against the BJP or issue a public apology within seven days," says BJP spokespers...

articles.title: Sahitya Akademi award-winning Tamil writer Poomani passes at 79
articles.sum: Poomani received the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2014 for his monumental novel Agnaadi, an epic narrative set against the backdrop of the Sivakasi (1899) and Kazhugumalai (1895) communal riots; Chief Minister Vijay announces State honours

articles.title: Suspended employee of Shri Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee arrested for donation 'irregularities'
articles.sum: Accused Pramod Nautiyal served as the Personal Assistant to the BKTC chairman.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari backs curbs on Kolkata airport mosque entry, cites security concerns
articles.sum: The location of the Kolkata international airport is critical since both China and Bangladesh are close by; it cannot keep its gates open for outsiders, said Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari

articles.title: Road rage | Politics of renaming a street in Bengal
articles.sum: Soon after the BJP government renamed Kolkata’s Suhrawardy Avenue as Gopal Mukherjee Road, there was public outcry — and historical evidence — that the street was originally named not after the man whom the government blames for killings, but rather the first Muslim V-C of Calcut...

articles.title: ISRO successfully carries out tests of Gaganyaan crew module systems
articles.sum: One of the tests involved separation of the ‘umbilical mechanism’ that serves as a link between the crew module, where astronauts live, and the service module that provides propulsion, says the agency

articles.title: BJP, RSS, VHP have hijacked Lord Ram, says former MP V. S. Ugrappa
articles.sum: He told reporters in Belagavi that none of the 11 members of the temple trust, including a retired government officer, were able to prevent the alleged theft

articles.title: SC rejects plea of 350 West Bengal madrasa teachers seeking grant-in-aid salaries
articles.sum: A few days ago, in a fresh order, the Suvendu government ordered an inspection of unaided madrasas operating across 12 districts and directed the authorities concerned to submit reports by July 21. The inspection is slated to be completed by July 15, an official said. Barely days...

articles.title: Badrinath donation theft case: Temple employee Pramod Nautiyal arrested
articles.sum: The action comes a day after the Uttarakhand government directed the chief executive officer of the Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee (BKTC) to initiate action against officials and employees found responsible for alleged financial irregularities, following an inquiry into pay...

articles.title: FSSAI wants you to be its eyes on ground
articles.sum: The FSSAI is encouraging citizens to report food safety violations. Consumers can now act as the regulator's eyes on the ground. Unsafe food and unhygienic kitchens can be reported through a dedicated platform. A single photograph can help authorities identify violations and take...

articles.title: Why apex court's wading into Darbhanga's ponds
articles.sum: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear a case concerning alleged encroachment of three historic water bodies. Petitioners accuse the Bihar government of threatening these lakes through a beautification project. Locals argue the government's plan constitutes encroachment disguised a...

articles.title: Dhar's Saraswati is Gayatri: Science ends 900-year mix-up
articles.sum: A 12th-century sculpture previously identified as Saraswati has been correctly identified as Gayatri. Digital documentation and 3D mapping revealed iconographic clues missed for centuries. The absence of a veena, a common Saraswati attribute, proved crucial in this re-identificat...

articles.title: Delivery agent barges into woman's Bengaluru flat, flashes her
articles.sum: A Bengaluru food vlogger reported a frightening incident involving a delivery agent. The agent allegedly forced his way into her washroom and exposed himself. Police arrested the 22-year-old delivery agent, Vijay Mallikarjun Kamat, following the complaint. Kamat claimed he only n...

articles.title: Tensions erupt in West Bengal after 11-year-old raped and murdered
articles.sum: The girl's body was fished out from a pond on Sunday - a day after her family reported her missing.
